channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: A court in Kenya blocks U.S. plan to keep American Ebola patients in Africa
item.dc:creator: Rael Ombuor, Lena H. Sun
item.description: A facility built by the U.S. military on a Kenyan air base was intended to isolate Americans exposed to Ebola during the growing outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: After a brutal winter, Ukraine’s drones are breaking Russian defenses
item.dc:creator: Steve Hendrix, Serhii Korolchuk
item.description: Russia’s advance has suddenly stalled, and Ukraine is fighting on its own terms — a comeback credited to Kyiv’s efforts to steadily strengthen the capabilities of its UAVs.

item.title: A condemnation of Putin in glitzy Cannes still angers Ukrainians besieged by war
item.dc:creator: Gerry Shih, Anastacia Galouchka
item.description: Andrei Zvyagintsev urged Russian President Vladimir Putin to end the “bloodbath” — but in Ukraine his remarks elicited ridicule and frustration.
